films.title: The Odyssey
films.review:
Powerful. I think that is the best way to describe this film. Powerful. It was only through immense power that this film was able to be executed. And, throughout it all, it is because of this power that The Odyssey captures you in its entirety for 3 hours.To burn the walls of Troy was to burn the world-entire. Including his homeNot once did I have an urge to pull out a phone or get up to pee, unlike the bulk of people walking around or on their phones. Nor did I bring my phone but that is a different story. The sheer power from the film, it's shots, the energy, and most notably, Goransson's soundtrack takes me to a completely different place. It brings me to tears to see a story and culture ingrained in me since childhood come to life. From Polyphemus to the Laestrygonians, to the Shades and the rage in Odysseus' return, every scene had an immense representation of power and scale, while also having emotional aspects which kept it grounded in humanity.

Thank you, Christopher Nolan, for keeping cinema alive.

I would like to point out this line from Homer's original text, Alcinous speaking:Explain to us also what sorrow makes you weep as you listen to the tragic story of the Argives and the fall of Troy. The gods were responsible for that, weaving catastrophe into men's lives to make a song for future generations.

films.title: Final Destination 3
films.review:
This review may contain spoilers.

Ramona Flowers went from this Scott Pilgrim to predicting the lives of 2 dozen teens on a roller coaster. What a glow up.

That tanning bed scene was so stressful, and transitioned well into the funeral. RIP.

Train crash was comparable to the Incredible Animated one, insane. 

Most gory and terrifying one yet. Enjoyed it thoroughly. Reminded me hella of say cheese and die, a childhood favorite from Goosebumps.

films.title: The Director's Notebook: The Cinematic Sleight of Hand of Christopher Nolan
films.review: 20 years of progress from this to the odyssey is truly present with Nolan's work

films.title: The Odyssey: The Making of an Epic
films.review: They kept saying the term ambition, and I think it was one of the highest qualities of ambition ever delivered. A large scale film that can touch the darkest parts of your heart, just what the world needed to keep film alive....the rigging that they did to basically turn [Polyphemus's Cave] into a natural soundstage... I don't know any other director who would try it

films.title: The Odyssey
films.review:
This review may contain spoilers.

Day 199 (I thought) of 2026. Opening Night of The Odyssey. Movie 200 of 2026.

And what a film to see. I loved every minute of it. Soundtrack had me hooked from the minute it started. I was a bit shaky at first, but honestly once it got going I was so engaged. I cried. I laughed. I jumped. An experience for the decades. With a soundtrack inspired by Tenet and shots inspired by The Prestige, Nolan definitely did this justice. Dare I even say: christopher nolan horror movie... Potentially a longer review to come.

films.title: Sinister
films.review: genuinely one of the most terrifying horrors I have seen in a while. Hokum(2026) definitely took a lot of inspiration from this; I love the way horror directors blend mystery, thrillers, and often a literary aspect. Not a ghost story, not a mystery, not a horror movie, but something derivative of all three. I quite liked the idea of explaining a mystery through a set of old films. Similar to how Weapons used a set of intertwined characters to engage the viewer into the mystery aspect of it. The sound design and use of shadows really kept me on edge too. Only complaint is that the acting from characters outside of Ethan Hawke seemed quite subpar.

films.title: Arrival
films.review: For a space movie hater, I seem to cry at a lot of them. Very happy to have watched this in the theatre experience. In depth review here.

films.title: Jinsei

films.title: The Conjuring
films.review:
watched this alone at night in a old creaky house and i am genuinely more stressed and scared than the four times i watched obsession (2025). 

in other news, everyone in this movie is hot including masc les Joey King, Ron Livingston, Patrick Wilson, and both their lives. cinematography was amazing (especially with the bed scene and the little 360 rotation of the camera). definitely too many jumpscares that were well done. lights on and doors open bro

films.title: Tuner
films.review:
This review may contain spoilers.

dare I say a sound masterpiece. emotionally touching, heart wrenching, a perfect film. would give it 5 stars if I were rating. when Niki jumped off the hat stage - so hot. And that sequence of the safe during Ruthie's performance - I did not breathe. Definitely a comfort film even with the crime aspect. I really like the fact that his hearing issue was not the first thing revealed. And we got a lot of background info before he actually started safe cracking.

films.title: The Batman
films.review:
wow. wasn't expecting to like this as much as I did. felt like a mystery being solved almost. Robert Pattinson is one tasty meal for sure (it's the hair). really liked the sequence of Zoe Kravitz with Pattinson in her ear. Gotham had hella world building in this for sure. Lighting was a big deal.

did not to be fucking 3 hrs tho my gosh

films.title: Under the Skin

films.review: The days are 67 hours long. The nights, 67 colder ones.I think I may be a fake Nolan fan because I don't remember watching this. And if I have, it must've been at a very young age. Insane for 3D people to be representing 5D space-time on a 2D TV screen. the watch artifact definitely took something from inception
